onboarding.step_fiscal_subtitle: Choose the month your financial year starts
onboarding.step_fiscal_question: When does your financial year start?
onboarding.step_fiscal_ae_locked: As a micro-entrepreneur, your financial year must follow the calendar year (January–December). This cannot be changed.
onboarding.step_fiscal_help: Most salons use the calendar year (January). You can choose a different month if your accountant recommends it.
onboarding.step_fiscal_non_jan_hint: Your {year} exercise will run from {start_month_name} {prev_year} to {end_month_name} {year}.

inputs.heading: Your Monthly Figures

results.empty_state: Enter values on the left to see your results

save.no_salon: Select a salon to save this bilan.

pilotage.title: Pilotage
pilotage.subtitle: Track your salon's profitability month by month
pilotage.no_salon: Select a salon to view your monthly reports.
pilotage.point_mort_subtitle: Total outgoings = break-even threshold to reach
pilotage.pm_cout_vie_perso_tooltip: Minimum amount you need each month to live on. Included in your break-even.
pilotage.cout_vie_perso_reset_confirm: Reset to salon default for this month?
pilotage.no_expenses: No expenses entered for this month.
pilotage.no_data_year: No data for this year. Start by entering a month.
pilotage.duplicate_modal_subtitle: Copy expenses and salaries to selected months
pilotage.duplicate_banner_body: Your typical month data has been duplicated across 12 months to give you a full annual forecast. Adjust each month's Revenue/Expenses as the year progresses to reflect actual business performance.
pilotage.empty_year_cta_title: Create your forecast year in one click
pilotage.empty_year_cta_body: By duplicating your data across 12 months, you won't need to re-enter recurring costs each month (rent, insurance…). You'll only need to update the Revenue/Expenses specific to each month.
pilotage.empty_year_cta_button: Duplicate my typical month across all 12 months
pilotage.empty_year_cta_error: Failed to generate the year. Please retry or contact support.
pilotage.empty_year_subtitle: Instead of filling in 12 months one by one, enter your typical month once — and copy everywhere.
pilotage.empty_year_historical_body: You haven't entered anything for this year. Go back to the current year to start your typical month.
pilotage.empty_year_future_body: We can copy your {prevYear} year as a starting point for {year}.
pilotage.expenses_helper: Important: enter your expenses as close to reality as possible. If you are starting out or need benchmarks,

salary.no_salaries: No employees to display for this month.
salary.benchmark_hint: France benchmark: payroll ≤ 55% of revenue · Ideal target: ≤ 40%

prix.title: Pricing Calculator
prix.subtitle: Calculate your cost-per-minute and set profitable prices for each service
prix.step1_title: Your costs for the whole year
prix.step1_help: Enter your total annual costs: payroll + rent + utilities + supplies + other expenses. You can pull this figure from your annual summary in Pilotage.
prix.cout_annuel_hint: Payroll + charges + rent + everything that goes out. Pull this from your year summary in Pilotage.
prix.majoration_hint: Eric recommends 10% on top of the pure cost, for safety and profit.
prix.majoration_tooltip: Enter the percentage of profit and safety margin you want. Bonuses are paid from profit. The safety margin covers unexpected costs (repairs, compensation, etc.). Typical range: 10–20%.
prix.cout_reel_hint: This is what it costs you per productive minute. Your prices must at minimum cover this.
prix.taux_occupation_hint: Of your working time, how much do you actually spend hairdressing? If 1h present = 30 min hairdressing + 30 min other, that's 50%.
prix.no_employees_help: The cost-per-minute calculator requires at least one employee (or the business owner) in your salon profile. Add your staff in Settings → Employees.
prix.no_services: No services — click 'Add a service' to get started.
prix.confirm_delete: Deactivate this service? It will no longer be included in calculations.
prix.section_forfaits_desc: Full-service packages including welcome, preparation and finishing
prix.addon_tooltip: Additional time is the time spent with the client that is added to the service time (booking, welcome, cloakroom, drink, diagnosis, checkout, workstation cleaning). On average this is 10 min / client. Only processing/waiting times (e.g. colour development) are excluded. Example: Colour package = 10 min addon + 30 min colour + 35 min shampoo/cut/blowdry = 75 min billed.
prix.import_disabled: Complete at least one Pilotage month to import your data
prix.methodology_text: The break-even price = cost-per-minute × total service duration. Below this price, the service is performed at a loss. The recommended price includes a 10% margin to cover contingencies and profit.

compta_page.title: My Accounting
compta_page.subtitle: Your online accounting, specialised for hairdressers — powered by Noly Compta
compta_page.not_subscribed_subtitle: Hairdresser accounting specialists manage your books at an affordable price
compta_page.trial_note: No commitment. Cancel anytime. 14-day free trial included.
compta_page.past_due_message: There is an issue with your payment. Update your payment method to keep access to your accounting.
compta_page.cancelled_message: Your subscription has been cancelled. You can re-subscribe at any time.
compta_page.feature_bilan: Annual balance sheet and tax return
compta_page.access_error: Unable to access accounting. Please try again in a few moments.
compta_page.what_is_desc: Full accounting managed by hairdressing sector experts, directly accessible from your ComCoi space.

coming_soon.title: Coming soon
coming_soon.message: Coming soon — this feature is arriving in a future version.

employees.title: My team
employees.subtitle: Manage employees and collaborators
employees.no_employees_hint: Add your team members to use profitability and pricing calculations.
employees.no_salon: Select a salon to manage its employees.
employees.weeks_per_year_tooltip: An employee is present on average 228 days/year, i.e. 45.6 weeks (52 weeks – 5 leave – 1.4 public holidays). This is Eric's reference. Adjust if the contract differs.
employees.cotisations_patronales_hint: Employer charges. Not applicable for directors (self-employed).
employees.taux_occupation_hint: Share of contracted time spent with clients. Default: 65% (Eric reference).
employees.deactivate_confirm_message: History is preserved. The employee will no longer appear in new monthly reports.
employees.prestataire_note: Independent contractor: no employer charges. The amount entered is the direct monthly cost invoiced to the salon.
employees.dirigeant_note: The director is self-employed — their contributions are managed directly with URSSAF. Their cost in break-even calculations uses a ×1.45 multiplier.
employees.tns_transparency_body: Based on your net salary of {net} €, we apply ~{rate}% TNS contributions, approximately {cotisations} €/month.
employees.tns_transparency_override_hint: Enter the rate provided by your accountant.
employees.dirigeant_contract_tooltip: TNS = self-employed (majority SARL, sole trader). Employed director = SASU, SAS, minority SARL. This determines how social charges are calculated. Ask your accountant if unsure.
employees.assimile_transparency_body: An employed director pays the same social charges as a regular employee (RGDU, unemployment, pension etc.), approximately 80% of gross.
employees.assimile_salary_hint: Enter the monthly gross salary for the employed director.
employees.salary_to_complete_cta: Salary to complete — click to edit
employees.contract_subtype_tooltip: The training level determines whether the apprentice is counted in Mes Prix productive minutes. CAP = non-productive (reception, shampooing). BP/BM = productive with a default 35% occupancy rate.
employees.contract_subtype_cap_note: A CAP apprentice is not counted in Mes Prix productive minutes — their cost is included in overheads but does not add billable capacity.
employees.contract_subtype_bp_bm_note: BP/BM apprentice: counted as productive in Mes Prix with a default 35% occupancy rate (adjustable in the field below).

primes.title: Bonus Calculator
primes.description: Calculate employee bonuses using the progressive tier system. Deficits are automatically carried forward from month to month.
primes.objectif_jour_hint: Daily amount the employee must achieve to cover their cost
primes.prime_tiers_description: Bonuses are calculated using progressive tiers on the excess above the target.
primes.no_employee_hint: Select an employee to calculate their monthly bonuses.
primes.no_salary_data_hint: The selected employee has no salary set. You can enter a daily target manually.
primes.info_text: Communauté Coiffure's bonus system uses a progressive scale: each employee has a daily target calculated from their salary cost. If they exceed their monthly target, they receive a bonus on the excess. Rates increase with the excess (10% on the first €600, 12% on the next €300, etc.). If an employee doesn't meet their target, the deficit is carried to the next month.
primes.empty_state_subtitle: Select an employee and a year to view the bonus grid.
primes.error_required: Please select an employee and enter a daily target
primes.bareme_hint: These bands apply to revenue earned above the employee's monthly target.
primes.bareme_min_rows: A scale must have at least one band.
primes.bareme_order_error: Thresholds must be in ascending order.
primes.monthly_hint: Enter the revenue generated by {name} this month — not the salon's total.
primes.objectif_card_hint: Amount {name} must earn each working day to cover their cost.
primes.objectif_from_seuil_none: No Salary Threshold calculation found. Run one first.
primes.no_employee_hint_new: Use the selector above to choose the employee whose bonuses you want to calculate.

taxes.title: Tax simulator
taxes.subtitle: Estimate your taxes and contributions based on your legal status.

business_type.label: Legal status
business_type.auto_micro_note: You are VAT-exempt: you do not collect or recover VAT.

taxes.versement_liberatoire: Pay your income tax at the same time
taxes.versement_liberatoire_desc: Instead of a May declaration, you pay a small percentage each quarter with your contributions. Advantageous if you pay little tax elsewhere. Rate: 1.7%.
taxes.acre_desc: ACRE is a reduction in URSSAF contributions in year one. 50% in early 2026, 25% from July 2026. Tick if you qualify.

nb_parts_ir.label: Your number of tax shares
nb_parts_ir.hint: 1 share = single. 1.5 = single parent with 1 child. 2 = couple. 2.5 = couple + 1 child.

tva_threshold_warning.title: Warning: VAT threshold nearby
tva_threshold_warning.body: Your turnover exceeds 90% of the 37,500 EUR threshold. Beyond this, you become VAT-registered.

summary.effective_rate: Percentage going to taxes and contributions

taxes.error_ca: Please enter a valid annual turnover (greater than 0)

breakdown.title: Where your money goes
breakdown.cfe_hint: A tax paid by all businesses. The amount depends on your area — we provide a simplified estimate.

seuil_salaire.title: Salary break-even
seuil_salaire.subtitle: How much revenue must this employee generate to cover their total cost?
seuil_salaire.salaire_brut_hint: The salary shown on their pay slip, before charges.
seuil_salaire.cotisations_patronales: What you pay on top of their net salary
seuil_salaire.taux_produits: Share of products in your revenue
seuil_salaire.taux_produits_hint: If you sell shampoos and accessories, enter their share. Eric recommends 10%.
seuil_salaire.taux_charges_fixes_hint: Rent, electricity, water, insurance. Eric recommends 25%.
seuil_salaire.pct_securite_hint: A reserve for unexpected expenses. Eric recommends 5%.
seuil_salaire.pct_benefice_hint: Above your costs, this is your profit. Eric recommends 10%.
seuil_salaire.semaines_an_hint: We count 5.2 weeks of leave on average, so 45.6 working weeks.
seuil_salaire.section_breakeven: How much they need to generate
seuil_salaire.marge_disponible: What's left to cover their salary
seuil_salaire.marge_disponible_hint: Once products and fixed costs are removed, here's what each euro collected can cover.
seuil_salaire.section_objective: Your target for them to be worthwhile
seuil_salaire.info_text: This calculator determines the minimum revenue an employee must generate to fully cover their cost (salary + charges). Available margin accounts for products and fixed charges. The final objective includes safety and profit margins.
seuil_salaire.empty_state: Enter employee parameters to see results
seuil_salaire.error_marge: Product share + fixed costs share must total less than 100%

marge_revente.title: Product resale margin
marge_revente.description: Calculate your selling price and margin from the purchase price and a multiplier coefficient.
marge_revente.prix_achat_label: What you paid for it (excl. VAT)
marge_revente.prix_achat_hint: The price you paid your supplier, excl. VAT.
marge_revente.coefficient_hint: Multiply your purchase price to set your selling price. ×2.5 = you sell at 2.5× what you paid.
marge_revente.empty_state_line1: Enter a purchase price and coefficient
marge_revente.info_text: The coefficient multiplies your purchase price to determine the selling price. A coefficient of 2.5 means you sell at 2.5× the purchase price, giving a 60% margin. The TTC price includes 20% VAT.
marge_revente.error_prix_achat: Please enter a valid purchase price (greater than 0)
marge_revente.error_coefficient: Please enter a valid coefficient (greater than 0)

volume_clients.title: Client Volume Calculator
volume_clients.description: Estimate how many clients you need to reach your revenue target, broken down by gender and month.
volume_clients.info_text: This calculator estimates the number of clients needed to reach your annual revenue target. It takes into account your client mix (women/men), average tickets, visit frequency and opening days to calculate a precise daily target.
volume_clients.objectif_ca: How much you want to make this year
volume_clients.pct_clients_f: What share of your clients are women?
volume_clients.montant_moyen_f: What a female client pays on average
volume_clients.montant_moyen_f_hint: Average visit amount — cut, colour, blow-dry… all included.
volume_clients.montant_moyen_h: What a male client pays on average
volume_clients.montant_moyen_h_hint: Average visit amount — cut, beard… all included.
volume_clients.nb_visites_f: How many times a year each female client comes back
volume_clients.nb_visites_f_hint: On average, a female client returns 4 times a year. Adjust for your salon.
volume_clients.nb_visites_h: How many times a year each male client comes back
volume_clients.nb_visites_h_hint: On average, a male client returns 6 times a year.
volume_clients.jours_semaine: How many days a week are you open?
volume_clients.semaines_an: How many weeks a year do you work?
volume_clients.unique_clients: How many different clients you need
volume_clients.simulation_title: What if you change your prices?
volume_clients.simulation_desc: Test the impact of changing your average tickets on your annual revenue.
volume_clients.sim_pct_f: If your women's ticket increases by
volume_clients.sim_pct_h: If your men's ticket increases by

about.meta_description: Discover the story of Communauté Coiffure — the financial management platform for independent hairdressers, built by a hairdresser for hairdressers.
about.headline: Who are we?
about.subtitle: The strength of community in service of your independence. A revolutionary and effective approach.
about.section1_p1: Communauté Coiffure was born out of an unbearable realisation: the stark lack of concrete solutions to turn artisanal skill into financial success.
about.section1_p2: After 40 years of experience, Éric, founder of Communauté Coiffure, observed that independent hairdressers — the backbone of our craft — were left to fend for themselves. Faced with the complexity of management, employment law and the pressure of profitability, there were no tools, no support, no structures to help them without stripping away their freedom. Éric lived that solitude himself and saw it in hundreds of colleagues.
about.section1_p3: He then understood there was only one solution: create it himself. Communauté Coiffure was born from that necessity. Éric built what existed nowhere else: a bridge between the demands and passion of an artisanal craft and financial success, designed by a salon owner for salon owners. Make no mistake: what we have built here is no pipe dream. It is the result of a lifetime dedicated to the trade. Éric puts at your service not only four decades of accumulated experience but also his exclusive network of experts — specialist lawyers, accountants, developers, business strategists, and supplier relationships. Everything that makes Communauté Coiffure strong is built on real foundations, tested and validated in practice.
about.section2_p1: Today, it is no longer possible to stay isolated. The complexity of the market demands professionalisation — without sacrificing what gives our craft its soul: our freedom and independence.
about.section2_p2: Communauté Coiffure is the tool that means you are no longer alone. We have built an ecosystem where expertise in management, accounting, legal and commercial matters becomes truly accessible and understandable.
about.section3_intro: We are not a franchise network. We are your partner in independence. Through our platform, you gain access to concrete management tools:
about.section3_item1_body: Calculate, anticipate, decide. Our break-even and payroll management tools make you a true business owner.
about.section3_item2_body: Stop leaving things to chance. From company formation to hiring questionnaires, we secure your foundations.
about.section3_item3_body: Digitalisation, visibility, working methods… We optimise what already exists to grow your margins mechanically. EARN MORE WITH WHAT YOU HAVE. IMMEDIATELY.
about.section4_title: Tomorrow, together, we will go further
about.section4_p1: Communauté Coiffure is a living organism. Each new member strengthens our capacity to act for the profession. If we are deploying cutting-edge management tools today, it is not only to 'secure' your day-to-day — it is to help you take a decisive step forward in profitability and quality of life.
about.section4_p2: The figures speak for themselves: while independent hairdressers are the most numerous, the 10% of salons under a brand capture over 30% of total hairdressing revenue. This imbalance is not inevitable — it is the result of an organisational gap we need to close.
about.section4_p3: We are not here to help you 'hang in there', but to give you the concrete levers that turn your work into real profits. Every feature we develop has one goal: increase your margin, consolidate your revenue and give you back the financial freedom your efforts and talent deserve. And this is only the beginning. Our ambition is to build an ever-stronger group. The more of us there are, the more collective weight we carry in transforming the conditions of our trade. In time, this shared strength will let us unlock savings and competitive advantages inaccessible to the individual.
about.section5_title: A new era for the profession
about.section5_p1: We are opening a new path for our craft: one where solidarity serves performance. At Communauté Coiffure, you remain free in your choices — but you never face your decisions alone.
about.section5_p2: Joining Communauté Coiffure means choosing to stop enduring your business and start steering it toward the success you are aiming for.
about.cta_text: Ready to take control of your salon's profitability?

footer.tagline: The financial management platform for independent hairdressers.

coco.greeting: Hello! I'm CoCo, the Communauté Coiffure assistant.
coco.greeting_sub: How can I help you today?

expenses.label: Label
expenses.label_is_annual_hint: Annual expense — divide your annual amount by 12 to get the monthly cost.
expenses.cfe_info_bubble: Exempt in the first year of business. Divide the annual amount by 12 to get the monthly cost. CFE is not subject to VAT (0% rate).
expenses.sacem_spre_info_bubble: SACEM and SPRE are paid annually — divide the annual amount by 12 to get the monthly cost (VAT 5.5%).
expenses.label_no_match: No label found — use 'Other' to enter manually

annual.card_sentence_near: Just above the threshold — watch the trend.

step4.urssaf_callout: URSSAF takes {urssaf_eur} — that's {urssaf_rate}% of your revenue.

wizard.step3_expense_empty_hint: Enter an amount or uncheck this line
wizard.step3_blocker_single: 1 active expense has no amount — enter an amount or uncheck it.
wizard.step3_blocker_plural: {n} active expenses have no amount — enter amounts or uncheck them.
wizard.step3_benchmark_explainer: The % benchmarks show the ideal share this expense should represent of your revenue (based on averages for the hairdressing sector)
wizard.step3_honoraires_help: Monthly TTC amount paid to your accountant. If you pay quarterly, divide by 3. Lets CoCo estimate potential savings with ComCoi Compta.
wizard.step3_payslip_help: TTC price per payslip you pay your current payroll provider (software or firm). Lets CoCo estimate potential savings with ComCoi Paie.
wizard.step3_nb_bulletins_help: By default we use your employee count. Update here if your actual number differs (apprentice, leave, director who changed status…).
wizard.step4_duplicate_explainer: Duplicating data saves you from re-entering recurring expenses each month (rent, subscriptions, loans, etc.). Only the current month's data will need correcting.
wizard.step4_duplicate_all_success: 12 months created — your year is ready

coco_cta.explain_aria: Ask CoCo to explain this result

lineage.estim_tooltip: Pre-filled from the typical month — not yet manually entered
lineage.partial_tooltip: Some months have been entered, others are still estimates

parametrage.majoration_tooltip: Enter the percentage of profit and safety margin you want. Bonuses are paid from profit. The safety margin covers unexpected costs (repairs, compensation, etc.). Typical range: 10–20%.
parametrage.mix_clientele_tooltip: Enter the share (%) of female and male clients. Visits/year = average number of visits per year per client.
parametrage.type_exploitant_tooltip: Determines how the owner's social contributions are calculated. AE: flat rate on revenue. TNS: ~45% of net salary. Assimilé salarié: employer contributions as for an employee.
parametrage.fiscal_year_tooltip: The financial year is the 12-month period on which your annual accounts are based. Most salons use the calendar year (Jan–Dec). Micro-entrepreneurs must use the calendar year.
